failure_surface: All code execution attempts failed with SyntaxError on line 1 due to malformed input containing non-Python characters (tool call markers like <tool_call>) embedded within the code block.
root_cause: The agent consistently produced code outputs that were contaminated with conversation/tool-call artifacts rather than clean executable Python. This prevented any actual spreadsheet manipulation logic from running, regardless of the correctness of the underlying algorithm.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before submitting code for execution, validate that the output contains only syntactically valid Python without extraneous markdown tags, tool-call wrappers, or conversation artifacts mixed into the code content.
evidence: Multiple execution attempts showed identical error: Cell In[1], line 1 shows <tool_call> ^ SyntaxError: invalid syntax
All four attempted solutions contained the same malformed opening with tool markers before import statements
No spreadsheet operations were ever performed since code never reached runtime
Test case results show [0] indicating zero passing tests due to complete execution failure

memory_items.title: Code Output Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Executable code submissions must contain only valid programming language syntax without embedded tool-call markers, conversation artifacts, or formatting noise that could cause parser failures.
memory_items.generalization: Always validate generated code for syntax errors before submission; strip any non-language characters from code blocks.

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Validation
memory_items.lesson: When the evaluation pipeline reports immediate syntax errors on line 1, treat this as an agent-side generation problem requiring correction of code formatting, not logic adjustment.
memory_items.generalization: Syntax errors prevent any downstream testing; fixing code cleanliness takes priority over business logic refinement when execution fails immediately.

memory_items.title: Clean Separation of Concerns
memory_items.lesson: Tool invocation protocols and executable code must remain distinctly separated; mixing structural markers with functional code creates unresolvable parsing conflicts.
memory_items.generalization: Design workflows where code generation produces isolated, standalone snippets that do not depend on or contain invocation wrapper syntax.

edits.content:
## Code Execution Integrity

- **Valid Syntax First**: Always verify generated code compiles before executing. Line-1 syntax errors indicate corrupted code blocks that require regeneration, not debugging.
- **Artifact-Free Output**: Code submissions must be free of non-language characters including tool-call wrappers, conversation markers, or markdown nesting that could break parsers.
- **Execution Pipeline Awareness**: Immediate syntax failures signal upstream generation problems. Prioritize code cleanliness over logic complexity when initial compilation fails.
